uranium_deficit_closed.detail: Goldman Sachs cumulative deficit 2025-2045 expanded from 1.703B to 1.914B lbs. Spot $77-80/lb, long-term contracts at $86/lb — utilities securing supply. Production under-delivery, mine restart delays, labor shortages, regulatory complexity. 'Price signals alone cannot resolve' the supply constraint.

nuclear_ppas_cancelled.detail: No cancellations. Meta 6.6GW, MSFT/AMZN/GOOG 10GW+ all proceeding. 2026 characterized as 'transformative' for uranium demand.

haleu_chokepoint_resolved.detail: Centrus DOE contract extended through June 2026 (Phase III, ~$110M). 900kg HALEU delivered in Phase II. Options for 8 additional years. BWXT named as deconversion partner. Pipeline building BUT still only Centrus enriching in US. Chokepoint narrowing but not eliminated.

micro_reactor_timeline_slipped.detail: TRISO fuel delivered to INL Dec 2025. EO 14299 deadline Sept 30, 2028. No evidence of schedule slip. Centrus contract extension confirms continued momentum.

natural_gas_demand_for_dc_collapsed.detail: 2026 = 'year of load accountability' for gas-fed data centers. Shift from headline MW to contract-backed MW. DTM acquired $1.2B in FERC pipelines. Guardian Pipeline 15% expansion for 2028. Demand accelerating, not collapsing.

goldman_deficit_expansion.detail: Goldman Sachs expanded cumulative uranium deficit forecast from 1.703B to 1.914B lbs (+12.4%). Target $91/lb by end 2026 — 20% upside from current ~$80.

centrus_phase_3.detail: DOE extended Centrus HALEU contract through June 2026 (Phase III, ~$110M). Options for 8 more years. BWXT named deconversion partner. This is the fuel supply chain for ALL advanced reactors materializing.

dtm_ferc_acquisition.detail: DTM acquired 3 FERC-regulated pipelines (3.7 Bcf/d, $1.2B) from ONEOK. Midwest market — data center power demand growth zone. CONFIRMS FGIP thesis: DTM capturing pipeline capacity ahead of DC buildout.

denison_breakout.detail: DNN +50.4% in 3 months. Wheeler River ISR deposit gaining attention as uranium deficit narrative strengthens. Strongest performer in uranium screen.

powl_infrastructure_leader.detail: POWL +53.4% in 3 months despite -10.4% pullback last month. Switchgear backlog 2-3 years. Serves data centers regardless of power source (solar, gas, nuclear). Power-agnostic infrastructure play.

screen_verdict.thesis_status: HOLDS — 0 of 5 falsification criteria met. HALEU chokepoint narrowing but not resolved.
screen_verdict.conviction: CONVICTION_4 (maintained). DTM elevated to CONVICTION_5 — $1.2B FERC acquisition confirms thesis.
screen_verdict.action: PAPER TRADE — NXE +40.7% and DNN +50.4% are the momentum leaders. LEU -8.4% 3M is the laggard despite being THE HALEU chokepoint — investigate. POWL pullback (-10.4% 1M) after +53.4% run — potential re-entry if backlog thesis holds.
